meta.title: OmicsLake Reproducibility Breakage Taxonomy
meta.objective: Classify reproducibility breakages and verify that OmicsLake can detect, explain, and safely resolve them.

scenarios.title: Input table silently replaced
scenarios.root_cause: Raw count values are replaced without explicit provenance updates.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: The change is often noticed only after downstream plots/tables diverge; root-cause search is manual and slow.

scenarios.title: Input schema/type drift
scenarios.root_cause: Input field type/shape changes and downstream summaries no longer match previous output semantics.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: Schema drift can pass silently when code coerces types; impact is discovered late.

scenarios.title: Analysis parameter changed
scenarios.root_cause: Filtering threshold is altered between runs without clear audit trail.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: Parameter provenance is dispersed across scripts/notebooks and difficult to compare.

scenarios.title: Critical intermediate removed or overwritten
scenarios.root_cause: A key derived artifact is removed/replaced during iterative analysis.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: Recovery depends on ad-hoc backups or rerunning large sections manually.

scenarios.title: Git provenance context missing
scenarios.root_cause: Execution occurs outside a valid Git repository context.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: Code state at analysis time cannot be reconstructed reliably.

scenarios.title: renv lockfile missing
scenarios.root_cause: Package lockfile is absent, so package versions cannot be pinned mechanically.
scenarios.conventional_bottleneck: Environment reconstruction relies on manual package history and memory.
